dailyPrompt.prompt: What's the most useful thing you learned this week?

featuredResponses.content: That my sourdough starter is more resilient than I thought. Left it unfed for two weeks and it bounced back in 24 hours. A good metaphor for most things in life.
Learned about the concept of 'negative space' in conversation — the pauses where real understanding happens. Changed how I run 1:1s at work.
That the word 'nostalgia' was coined as a medical diagnosis — Swiss soldiers literally got sick from missing home. Language holds so much history.
